certificate.moduleName: Rocky Linux 8 and 9 libgcrypt Cryptographic Module
certificate.caveat: When operated in approved mode and installed, initialized and configured as specified in Section 11 of the Security Policy. No assurance of minimum security of SSPs (e.g., keys, bit strings) that are externally loaded, or of SSPs established with externally loaded SSPs.
certificate.description: The Rocky Linux 8 and 9 libgcrypt Cryptographic Module is a general purpose crypto library based on the code used in GNU Privacy Guard.

services.name: Non- approved Mode
services.description: Automatically entered whenever a non-approved service is requested
services.indicator: Equivalent to the indicator of the requested service (the service API call return is not GPG_ERR_NO_ERROR)

services.name: RSA
services.approvedFunctions: Signature generation (pre-hashed); Signature verification (pre-hashed); Asymmetric encryption (primitive); Asymmetric decryption (primitive)

services.indicator: AES encryption using non-approved AES modes

services.name: Digital signature generation primitive
services.indicator: Generate a signature using ECDSA/RSA signature generation primitive

services.name: Digital signature verification primitive
services.indicator: Verify a signature using ECDSA/RSA signature verification primitive

services.name: Asymmetric encryption primitive
services.indicator: Perform encryption using RSA encryption primitive

services.name: Asymmetric decryption primitive
services.indicator: Perform decryption using RSA decryption primitive

services.name: Error State
services.description: The module will return an error code to indicate the error and will enter the Error state. Any further cryptographic operation is inhibited.
services.roleAccess: Failure of pre- operational tests or conditional tests.
services.indicator: An error message related to the cause of the failure.

extraColumns.col6: The error can be recovered by a restart (i.e., powering off and powering on) of the module.

services.name: Fatal Error state
services.description: The module will abort and will not be available.
services.roleAccess: Random numbers are requested in the error state or cipher operations are requested on a deallocated handle.

extraColumns.col6: The error can be recovered by a restart (i.e., powering off and powering on) of the module.

The module will return an error code to indicate the error and will enter the Error state. Any further cryptographic operation is inhibited.
Failure of pre- operational tests or conditional tests.
The error can be recovered by a restart (i.e., powering off and powering on) of the module.
An error message related to the cause of the failure.
The module will abort and will not be available.
Random numbers are requested in the error state or cipher operations are requested on a deallocated handle.
The error can be recovered by a restart (i.e., powering off and powering on) of the module.
